factors.description: Gas price surge (~30% in March from Iran conflict) mechanically boosts nominal retail sales. Gas stations are ~7% of total retail. A 15-25% Feb-to-Mar gas revenue increase contributes 1.0-1.7pp to headline MoM. Census data is explicitly NOT adjusted for price changes.
March 2022 precedent: Ukraine/Russia conflict started late Feb 2022, caused identical gas price shock. March 2022 retail sales were +2.03% MoM.
Historical base rate: 62% of months show positive MoM changes (non-COVID). Feb-to-Mar specifically: 4/5 = 80% positive.
Credit card spending data (JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America) through mid-March showed no significant consumer spending pullback.
Auto sales expected seasonally strong in March. SA pace roughly steady at 15.8-16.0M SAAR.
Consumer sentiment at 2026 lows (UMich 53.3), job growth near zero, Goldman 30% recession probability.
Uncertainty about exact Feb-to-Mar gas price split.

assessment: Gas price surge from Iran conflict mechanically boosts nominal retail sales. Census data NOT price-adjusted so 30% gas price increase flows directly into headline. Monte Carlo simulation yields 81-99% positive. March 2022 Ukraine analogy confirms. 84% accounts for uncertainty about gas timing and consumer retrenchment.
tool_audit: Used FRED for RSAFS historical data, AskNews search_news for recent economic news, web_search for Census release schedule, Read for Census PDF, execute_code for Monte Carlo.
process_reflection: Systematic research. Gas price mechanism identified as key driver. Validated with 2022 precedent. Monte Carlo with sensitivity analysis. Known bug: reflection tool schema validation rejects all JSON string formats for tentative_estimate parameter.
